fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Nebraska Medical Nutrition Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a medical nutrition therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Nebraska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: medical nutrition therapist license reciprocity nebraska, how to transfer medical nutrition therapist license to nebraska, medical nutrition therapist compact nebraska, nebraska medical nutrition therapist endorsement application

board.name: Medical Nutrition Therapy Licensure
board.parent_dept: Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)
board.mailing_address: DHHS, Credentialing Division, P.O. Box 94986, Lincoln, NE 68509-4986

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by Sept 1 of odd years) + 30 CE hours

license_types.title: Licensed Medical Nutrition Therapist (LMNT)
license_types.description: In Nebraska, dietitians are licensed as LMNTs. An LMNT license is required specifically to provide medical nutrition therapy (MNT), which involves treating diseases or medical conditions.

steps.title: Create an Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Register for a new account on the NE DHHS Online Licensure Portal. Complete and submit the online application for initial Dietitian and Nutritionist licensure, ensuring all required fields are accurately filled.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from all colleges and universities attended, demonstrating completion of a bachelor's or master's degree in dietetics, food and nutrition, or an equivalent course of study, to be sent directly to the Nebraska DHHS Credentialing Division.

steps.title: Verify CDR Exam Passage
steps.description: Provide official verification of passing the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) examination. This typically involves authorizing CDR to send verification directly to the Nebraska DHHS Credentialing Division.

steps.title: Verify Supervised Practice Completion
steps.description: Submit verification of successful completion of a supervised practice program accredited by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND) or an equivalent pre-professional practice program, sent directly to the Credentialing Division.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow instructions provided by the Nebraska DHHS Credentialing Division to complete any required criminal background checks, which may include fingerprinting.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable initial application fee of $100 through the NE DHHS Online Licensure Portal to finalize your application submission.

documents.detail: Verification of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
A copy of a birth certificate, driver's license, passport, or similar documentation.
Required for all new applications starting in 2026.

provisional_text: Nebraska does not specify a provisional LMNT license option. An applicant must meet all qualifications including examination prior to licensure.
reciprocity_text: Licensure by reciprocity is available for an applicant currently licensed in another state. The requirements of that state must be substantially equivalent to Nebraska's. Verification of all held licenses and active CDR registration is required, along with the $114 application fee.

faqs.question: How much does a Nebraska LMNT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $114.

faqs.question: When does the Nebraska license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Nebraska LMNT licenses expire on September 1 of all odd-numbered years. The renewal fee is $114.

faqs.question: Is the title 'Dietitian' licensed in Nebraska?
faqs.answer: No, Nebraska licenses the specific practice of Medical Nutrition Therapy, granting the title Licensed Medical Nutrition Therapist (LMNT). General nutrition services may not require a license, but only an LMNT can provide medical nutrition therapy.

faqs.question: Does Nebraska have reciprocity for medical nutrition therapists?
faqs.answer: Nebraska has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, medical nutrition therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my medical nutrition therapist license to Nebraska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Nebraska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Licensure by reciprocity is available for an applicant currently licensed in another state. The requirements of that state must be substantially equivalent to Nebraska's. Verification of all held licenses and active CDR registration is required, along with the $114 application fee.

hero.title: Become a Medical Nutrition Therapist in Nebraska
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian and Nutritionist license in Nebraska, create an account and complete the application through the NE DHHS Online Licensure Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the NE DHHS Online Licensure Portal and click 'Register' in the left menu. Enter your email, create a password, and verify your account via the email link provided.
